narrative: Dear Alex, imagine our admissions model as a wise mentor who's reviewed thousands of stories like yours, piecing together patterns from past students to spot who thrives on the bar exam journey. It looked at your path and saw a 46% chance of stumbling at that final hurdle, which tipped the scales against your application—it's a tough call, but one rooted in the details of your profile. The biggest signal came from your Year 3 decile of 2.0, placing you near the bottom of your class then, which echoed strongly as a pull toward struggling with the bar's demands. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 3.0, suggesting early challenges in law school that often snowball for others in similar spots. Your LSAT score of 34, while solid for some, stood out here as another nudge toward difficulty, as it hasn't always been the launchpad for bar success in profiles like yours. Layered on top was your upper-middle family income, which surprisingly correlated with hurdles for students in your bracket—perhaps less financial pressure doesn't always translate to the grit needed. Your undergrad GPA of 2.1 added to that picture, hinting at foundational academic hurdles that the bar amplifies. Even factors like being a white male pursuing full-time study wove in as subtle drags, based on how those traits have played out historically in the data. It's not that any one piece defines you—it's this combination painting a cautious tale of risk. You've got real potential, Alex; many have turned similar starts around with targeted effort. Consider bolstering those deciles or prepping intensely for the bar—your story isn't written yet. We're rooting for you to rewrite it.
